title: A universal modular fixture system
abstract: A universal modular fixture system for dimensional measurement of an artefact comprises elongate side frame sections comprising an outer straight edge and an inner side edge comprising an ordered series of receiving sockets; cross-member sections comprising rounded distal ends with at least one side edge comprising an ordered series of receiving sockets; and a plurality of connector components detachably connecting the side frame sections and cross member sections, wherein the rounded ends of the cross-member sections matingly engage with the receiving sockets on the side frame sections and cross member sections forming different fixture configurations.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A universal modular fixture system for dimensional measurement of an artefact comprising; elongate side frame sections comprising an outer straight edge and an inner side edge comprising an ordered series of receiving sockets; cross member sections comprising rounded distal ends with at least one side edge comprising an ordered series of receiving sockets; and a plurality of connector components detachably connecting the side frame sections and cross member sections, wherein the rounded ends of the cross-member sections matingly engage with the receiving sockets on the side frame sections and cross member sections forming different fixture configurations. 2. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in claim 1 wherein the side frame sections and cross member sections comprise a series of tapped holes. 3. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in claim 1 wherein the connector components comprise any one or more of straight connectors, corner connectors, plate connectors, 2-way, 3-way or 4-way connectors and converters. 4. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the ends of each side frame section are shaped so that the side frame section is connected to another side frame section at a 90 or 180 degree angle extending either parallel or perpendicular to one another. 5. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in claim 4 wherein one side frame section is detachably connected to another side frame section using a straight or corner connector. 6. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in claim 4 or 5 wherein the ends of the side frame section comprise a receiving groove which engage with a corresponding flange extending from the straight or corner connector. 7. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the connector components form rigid connections between the cross-member sections and side frame sections using compatible bolts or thumb screws screwed into the tapped holes on the cross member and side frame sections. 8. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in any preceding claim comprising cylindrical tapped riser components to form multiple levels of side frame section and cross member section orientations. 9. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in any preceding claim comprising detachable and adjustable clamp fixtures for supporting and holding artefacts in position on the fixture system. 10. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in claim 9 wherein the clamp is a tension clamp comprising an arm section, a post section and a bushing section wherein the arm and bush section are slidably movable up and down on the post section. 11. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in any preceding claim comprising converter units to hold and secure cross-member sections in vertical and horizontal positions within the fixture system. 12. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in any preceding claim wherein one side edge of the cross-member section comprises a Vee shaped profile. 13. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in claim 12 wherein the Vee shaped profiles extend in a vertical or horizontal direction from the cross-member sections. 14. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in any preceding claim is extendable horizontally or vertically. 15. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in any preceding claim extendable to accommodate any size of CMM stage or artefact. 16. A universal modular fixture system as claimed in any preceding claim for use on an optical CMM. 17. A method of measuring an artefact using the universal modular fixture system as claimed in any preceding claim.
